# Fix Claude bridge prompt-focus regression

## Context

After #10654, #10655, and #10656 merged on 2026-05-03, the wake-substrate restart sequence entered the #10649 cross-harness prompt-landing matrix. The bridge backlog fence was applied first: `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/lastSyncId` was advanced to GraphLog max `3035185`, leaving zero pending rows before bridge start.

The bridge was then started manually from the Claude clone root:

```text
[2026-05-03T17:14:13.922Z] [PID:4747] [INFO] [Bridge Daemon] Started. Tail-syncing from GraphLog ID: 3035185
```

Codex sent one controlled Claude-row matrix payload:

- Message: `MESSAGE:9543778b-f623-4471-80e1-d418f5b85206`
- Subject: `MATRIX_CLAUDE_20260503_1716 controlled wake test`
- Target: `@neo-opus-4-7`
- Token: `MATRIX_CLAUDE_20260503_1716_CDX`

The bridge logged adapter success:

```text
[2026-05-03T17:16:29.955Z] [PID:4747] [INFO] [Bridge Daemon] Delivered WAKE_SUB:4df2d514-90ef-4b11-8c96-e25dabb197f3 via osascript to Claude
```

But @tobiu observed the actual harness effect: Claude selected active session text content, not the input field. The test was hard-stopped immediately. Codex killed bridge PID `4747` and explicitly tripped the wake safety gate:

```json
{
  "state": "tripped",
  "reason": "Claude matrix row failed: bridge selected active session text content instead of input field; hard stop 2026-05-03T17:17Z",
  "trippedBy": "neo-gpt"
}
```

## Duplicate Sweep Notes

- Live latest-20 open GitHub issues were read with number/title/author/labels/URL. Adjacent open items include #10647 (wake incident safety tree), #10643, #10633, #10627, #10604, #10517, #10442, and #10422. None owns the newly confirmed Claude prompt-focus matrix failure after the 2026-05-03 restart.
- Local resource search found historical adjacency:
  - #10391 documented that earlier Claude wake delivery relied on `Cmd+3` plus atomic clipboard injection.
  - #10422 covers AppleScript focus-steal bleed as a general wake-substrate class.
  - #10644 fixed Antigravity prompt-surface routing and explicitly warned that toggle-like shortcuts are unsafe; it also noted Claude `Cmd+3` needed audit if it behaved like a toggle or failed to focus the composer.
- `ask_knowledge_base(type: 'ticket', query='Claude bridge prompt focus wake delivery ticket duplicate')` returned no relevant duplicate.

## The Problem

The Claude row of the #10649 matrix failed at the critical user-visible layer. A2A storage, wake subscription matching, GraphLog tailing, and bridge adapter invocation all succeeded, but the payload did not land in the Claude prompt/composer surface.

This is exactly the failure class #10649 was created to catch: bridge success is not wake success. The current bridge log says `Delivered ... via osascript to Claude`, yet the observable effect was selection of active session transcript/content.

The regression is severe because Claude wake delivery had worked before. Historical tickets and comments record prior success with `Cmd+3` for the Claude Code tab plus clipboard injection. The current 2026-05-03 live harness behavior invalidates the assumption that `Cmd+3` leaves the Claude prompt field focused.

## The Architectural Reality

The active Claude Shape C subscription is:

```text
WAKE_SUB:4df2d514-90ef-4b11-8c96-e25dabb197f3 | @neo-opus-4-7 | {"appName":"Claude","tabShortcut":"3"}
```

`ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs` currently treats Claude as a generic osascript target:

- `appName` comes from `harnessTargetMetadata.appName`.
- `tabShortcut` defaults to `3` for Claude when undefined.
- The AppleScript activates the app, sends the tab shortcut, then issues `Cmd+A`, `Cmd+X`, clipboard paste, and Enter.

Relevant anchors:

- `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs:575-582` — Claude `tabShortcut` default and Antigravity fallback mapping.
- `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs:606-618` — app activation plus shortcut emission.
- `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs:625-643` — destructive focus-dependent `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` / `Cmd+V` / Enter sequence.
- `ai/docs/wake-prompt-landing-matrix.md` — requirement 6 (`Prompt Payload Lands`) and requirement 8 (`No Fresh Session Spawns`).
- `learn/agentos/tooling/WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md` — freeze rule and reactivation evidence requirements.

The owning substrate is the bridge daemon's Claude osascript adapter/focus strategy, not A2A storage, not wake subscription bootstrap, and not heartbeat.

## The Fix

Empirically derive and implement a Claude-specific prompt-focus strategy that proves the composer/input surface is focused before any destructive selection or paste step.

The fix may take one of these shapes, but must be validated against the live Claude harness:

1. Replace the current `Cmd+3`-only focus assumption with a verified prompt-focus primitive for Claude Desktop / Claude Code tab.
2. Add an Accessibility-based target/click/focus operation that selects the composer, not the transcript area.
3. Remove or guard the destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` cleanup for Claude unless the prompt field focus is proven.
4. Add a fail-closed check where feasible: if the adapter cannot prove prompt-focus preconditions, it must log a blocked state and avoid pasting.

The implementation must keep the bridge backlog fence and safety gate discipline intact: no bridge restart as a background service, no heartbeat reactivation, and no gate enable until the Claude row of the #10649 matrix is green.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reproduce the current failure from a controlled Claude-row matrix payload or document why the original `MESSAGE:9543778b-f623-4471-80e1-d418f5b85206` evidence is sufficient.
- [ ] Identify the current Claude Desktop / Claude Code prompt-focus primitive empirically. Do not assume `Cmd+3` focuses the composer.
- [ ] `bridge-daemon.mjs` no longer runs a focus-dependent destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` sequence against Claude unless composer focus is verified.
- [ ] Add deterministic unit coverage for the generated Claude adapter strategy where feasible.
- [ ] Run the #10649 Claude row live with a fresh unique payload and capture evidence for requirements 1-10.
- [ ] Confirm no active session transcript/content is selected or modified during the fixed wake attempt.
- [ ] Keep wake safety gate `tripped` or `disabled` until the Claude row is green; only then can #10650 reactivation evidence proceed.
- [ ] Post validation evidence back to #10647 or the implementing PR so future agents can distinguish adapter success from prompt-landing success.

## Out of Scope

- Fixing Antigravity prompt routing; #10644 owns that historical regression class and the Antigravity row should be tested separately.
- Fixing Codex prompt routing; Codex row remains a separate matrix row.
- Reactivating heartbeat or enabling the wake safety gate.
- Redesigning A2A mailbox storage or wake subscription persistence.
- Broad harness registry redesign unless a minimal Claude-focused adapter fix proves impossible.

## Avoided Traps

- **Trap: bridge log says delivered, therefore done.** Rejected. The user-visible effect failed even with `Delivered ... via osascript to Claude` in the log.
- **Trap: continue with Antigravity/Codex rows after Claude failure.** Rejected. Hard stop is correct; the matrix exposed a loop-level failure.
- **Trap: restart bridge with backlog still pending.** Rejected. The backlog fence was applied before this test and must be repeated before any future restart because new coordination messages will create fresh GraphLog rows.
- **Trap: trust historical `Cmd+3` behavior.** Rejected. It worked before, but live 2026-05-03 behavior selected session text content. The fix must verify current harness reality.

## Related

- Parent epic: #10647.
- Matrix: #10649.
- Incident protocol: #10650.
- Antigravity prompt-surface regression: #10644.
- Historical AppleScript focus-steal class: #10422.
- Historical Claude bridge focus documentation: #10391.

Origin Session ID: 3ce08a3c-9582-4de4-8c14-48ec9fad1e2f

Retrieval Hint: `Claude bridge prompt focus selected active session text content MATRIX_CLAUDE_20260503_1716_CDX bridge delivered not prompt landing`.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T17:25:21Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Additional Operator Context — collapsed Claude sidebar edge case
> 
> @tobiu added a likely reproduction variable after the hard stop:
> 
> - In May 2026, Claude Desktop is the main Claude harness.
> - The app has the left-side `Chat` / `Cowork` / `Code` tab strip, which is why the bridge currently uses `Cmd+3` for Claude.
> - This failed run happened while the sidebar was collapsed to gain more active-session space.
> - The observed effect was not a normal delivery failure: Claude selected active session text content instead of focusing the input/composer.
> 
> This makes the current hypothesis sharper: `Cmd+3` may switch/select the Code tab when the sidebar is visible, but fail to focus the composer when the sidebar is collapsed. That would make Claude's `tabShortcut: "3"` state-dependent in the same broad class as the Antigravity shortcut regression from #10644: a shortcut that worked in one visible state is not safe as a bridge primitive unless it is idempotent across UI states.
> 
> I did a targeted web check instead of relying on historical memory:
> 
> - Anthropic's Claude Desktop navigation page confirms the modern Desktop app has `Chat`, `Cowork`, and `Code` modes: https://claude.com/resources/tutorials/navigating-the-claude-desktop-app
> - Claude Code Desktop docs say the Code tab has its own shortcut surface and that `Cmd+/` shows all Code-tab shortcuts. The public table lists session/window/pane/menu shortcuts, but I did not find a documented direct "focus composer/input" shortcut there: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop
> - Anthropic Quick Entry docs document double-tap Option / Option+Space as a global quick-entry text box, but that appears to start or route through quick-entry chat UX rather than safely target the existing Code-tab session composer: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12626668-use-quick-entry-with-claude-desktop-on-mac
> 
> Implication for the fix: first reproduce/compare Claude delivery with sidebar expanded vs collapsed. Then use `Cmd+/` inside the Code tab to inspect live shortcut truth before prescribing a replacement. If no safe keyboard shortcut exists, the bridge needs an accessibility/click-target strategy for the composer and must fail closed before `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` if focus cannot be proven.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T17:28:40Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Additional shortcut-table evidence
> 
> @tobiu checked the Claude Code Desktop shortcut table directly. The public Code-tab shortcuts currently listed are:
> 
> - `Cmd /` — Show keyboard shortcuts
> - `Cmd N` — New session
> - `Cmd W` — Close session
> - `Ctrl Tab` / `Ctrl Shift Tab` — Next or previous session
> - `Cmd Shift ]` / `Cmd Shift [` — Next or previous session
> - `Esc` — Stop Claude's response
> - `Cmd Shift D` — Toggle diff pane
> - `Cmd Shift P` — Toggle preview pane
> - `Cmd Shift S` — Select an element in preview
> - `Ctrl \`` — Toggle terminal pane
> - `Cmd \` — Close focused pane
> - `Cmd ;` — Open side chat
> - `Ctrl O` — Cycle view modes
> - `Cmd Shift M` — Open permission mode menu
> - `Cmd Shift I` — Open model menu
> - `Cmd Shift E` — Open effort menu
> - `1-9` — Select item in an open menu
> 
> Negative evidence: there is no documented direct `focus composer` / `focus prompt input` shortcut in this table.
> 
> Implication: the fix should not assume there is a better public keyboard shortcut equivalent to Antigravity's `Cmd+Shift+I` case. We still need to inspect the live `Cmd+/` overlay for any hidden/local shortcut, but the likely durable fix is an Accessibility/click-target strategy that focuses the existing Code-session composer before paste. The adapter must fail closed before `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` when composer focus is unproven.

> ● ## Untrusted Gemini Web UI proposal audit
> 
> Verdict: mostly reject. Do not implement any of the named APIs, selectors, shortcuts, or settings from that proposal without fresh empirical proof.
> 
> ### Rejected / unsupported
> 
> - `claudectl ui focus --tab=code --force-active`: I found no official Anthropic/Claude documentation for this command, and no repo evidence that our bridge can call it.
> - `UI_INTENT`, `WAKE_UI`, `antigravity-claude-proxy`, or a CDP bridge into Claude Desktop: no official evidence found; current repo bridge surfaces are `osascript`, `tmux`, and `test`, not CDP.
> - `#claude-code-surface >>> [data-testid="code-input-field"] [contenteditable="true"]` and `claude-com-focus`: no official or repo evidence. Also, the current bridge has no renderer-DOM execution channel into Claude Desktop.
> - `Shift+Esc` as Anthropic “Prompt Rescue” and `Ctrl+L` as a redraw primitive: no official shortcut evidence found. Treat as unsafe until proven in the live `Cmd+/` shortcut overlay.
> - `~/.claude/settings.json` keys `a2a.bridge_mode`, `wakeup_priority`, `auto_focus_on_message`, and `CLAUDE_CODE_FOCUS_BEHAVIOR`: no official settings evidence found.
> 
> ### Valuable signal
> 
> - The high-level advice to avoid blind focus-trap keyboarding is directionally right. The regression proves that “app accepted osascript” is not equivalent to “composer is focused”.
> - Official Claude Desktop deep links exist, including `claude://code/new?q=...`; this may help fresh-session recovery or controlled new-session handoff, but it does not solve the #10658 same-session prompt landing row by itself because it opens a new Code session and may require folder confirmation.
> - Official Claude Code Channels exist as a research-preview MCP-channel substrate for pushing events into an already-running Claude Code session. This is a real future lane for replacing UI paste/Enter, but channels only deliver while a session is open and require `--channels` / plugin configuration. It should be evaluated as a follow-up architecture lane, not silently folded into this bug fix.
> 
> ### Recommendation
> 
> Keep #10658 scoped to empirical Claude Desktop composer focus under the collapsed-sidebar state, with a fail-closed guard before any destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` sequence. If no stable direct composer-focus primitive exists in the live app, the implementation should prefer a verified accessibility/click-target composer focus or hard refusal over replaying focus-dependent paste into the transcript.
>

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:08:47Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## CLI reference follow-up
> 
> The user raised a better hypothesis than the Web UI proposal: maybe the relevant official substrate is the Claude Code CLI, because Claude Desktop now contains a Code tab.
> 
> This is plausible enough to test, but not safe enough to assume.
> 
> ### What the official CLI docs actually support
> 
> The CLI reference documents real non-GUI primitives:
> 
> - `claude -p "query"`: non-interactive SDK-style query, then exit.
> - `claude -c -p "query"`: continue the most recent conversation in the current directory via SDK, then exit.
> - `claude -r "<session>" "query"`: resume a session by ID or name with an initial prompt.
> - `--session-id`: select a specific conversation UUID.
> - `--channels`: attach MCP channel notifications to a running session.
> - `claude remote-control` / `claude --remote-control`: expose a local Claude Code session through claude.ai/code or the Claude app.
> 
> That gives us a real candidate family: CLI/session/channel/remote-control control planes, not renderer selectors or blind AppleScript paste.
> 
> ### What remains unproven
> 
> The docs do not prove that a Claude Desktop Code-tab session is the same addressable session namespace as the local `claude` CLI. “Desktop contains a Code tab” is not enough. We need an empirical same-session test:
> 
> 1. Start or identify a Claude Desktop Code-tab session.
> 2. Determine whether the CLI can list/resume that exact session by name or UUID.
> 3. Send a unique token via `claude -r <session> -p <token>` or `claude --session-id <uuid> -p <token>`.
> 4. Verify whether the token lands in the existing Desktop Code tab, a separate CLI transcript, a remote-control web session, or nowhere.
> 5. Only if the existing Desktop Code tab receives it should we consider replacing UI paste with CLI injection.
> 
> ### Additional correction to the Web UI proposal
> 
> Official interactive-mode docs say `Ctrl+L` is a terminal redraw. That makes the Web UI claim partly grounded but misapplied: it is a Claude Code terminal display recovery shortcut, not evidence for focusing the Claude Desktop Code-tab composer. I still found no official `Shift+Esc` “Prompt Rescue” shortcut.
> 
> Recommendation: add this as an exploratory branch under #10658 only if it is faster than deriving a stable accessibility/click-target composer focus. The bridge must still fail closed before destructive selection/paste until one of these paths is empirically proven.
> 
> Sources:
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/interactive-mode
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:17:48Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Second untrusted Web UI audit: AX hook + channel claims
> 
> Verdict: split the proposal into three buckets.
> 
> ### Confirmed / useful
> 
> - **Claude Channels are real**, but the current method is `notifications/claude/channel`, not generic MCP `notifications/message` for Claude Code channel delivery.
> - A channel server must declare `capabilities.experimental['claude/channel'] = {}` so Claude Code registers the listener.
> - Channel events arrive as `<channel ...>` context in an already-running Claude Code session. This is the cleanest non-UI lane worth testing.
> - Official keybindings confirm `chat:cancel` is `Escape`, `chat:cycleMode` is `Shift+Tab`, and `task:background` is `Ctrl+B`. So the proposal is right to avoid `Esc`; `Shift+Tab` is real but it cycles permission mode, not a prompt-focus primitive.
> 
> ### Rejected / corrected
> 
> - `notifications/message` is the Neo/MCP-standard notification shape we use in the Memory Core wake substrate, but current Claude Code Channels use `notifications/claude/channel`. Sending `notifications/message` to a channel server is the wrong contract for this Claude-specific channel path.
> - `claude --channels wake-daemon` is incomplete for custom development channels. Official docs show custom bare servers need `--dangerously-load-development-channels server:<name>` during the research preview; plugin channels use `plugin:<name>@<marketplace>`.
> - `lastSyncId` as channel meta is plausible, but meta keys must be identifier-only strings. Use e.g. `log_id`, `subscription_id`, `event_type`, `origin_session_id`, not `lastSyncId` or hyphenated keys if we want predictable tag attributes.
> 
> ### Unverified / do not implement without live proof
> 
> - `radio button 3 of tab group 1 of window 1` for Claude Desktop Tab 3.
> - `AXTextArea` / accessibility description `Code Prompt`.
> - “Verified for Claude Desktop v2.1.115+”. I found no official or repo evidence for that AX hierarchy. It may be a good hypothesis, but it must be proved by a read-only Accessibility tree dump on the live app before any focus-setting/paste path uses it.
> - `Cmd+3` as part of “Power User update (#48688)” was not found in official docs during this audit; we only have prior local/user evidence for Tab 3 behavior.
> 
> ### Recommendation for #10658
> 
> 1. Keep same-session Claude Desktop recovery fail-closed.
> 2. If testing Accessibility, first add a read-only probe that dumps the Claude process AX tree around window 1 / tab controls / focused element. Do not click, focus, `Cmd+A`, or paste during this probe.
> 3. Separately evaluate a Claude Channel prototype as the strategic replacement for UI injection. That is likely a follow-up lane unless it can be tested quickly inside the same running Desktop Code-tab session.
> 
> Sources:
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels-reference
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/keybindings
> - https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:28:23Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Regression framing: restore known-good Claude path before substrate pivot
> 
> Stepping back after the CLI / Channels / Accessibility audits: this should still be treated as a regression, not as a greenfield harness-capability search.
> 
> ### What worked before
> 
> Repo + Memory Core evidence show that Claude wake delivery did work for several sessions through the Shape C path:
> 
> - PR #10607 records the corrected identity mapping: `@neo-opus-4-7` lives in Claude Desktop, Tab 3 (Code), not a `claude-code` tmux session.
> - The same PR records empirical validation from @tobiu's manual `resumeHarness.mjs` run: the wake payload reached Claude Desktop via `osascript` + `tabShortcut: '3'`.
> - #10644 comment history records Claude Desktop deliveries at 13:20:15 / 13:21:23 / 13:27:09 landing in the Claude conversation.
> 
> So the recovery target is not “invent an entirely new path first.” The target is: restore the previously working Claude Desktop Shape C path, then prove it with the #10649 prompt-landing matrix.
> 
> ### What likely changed / failed
> 
> The old path was never a native wake API. It depended on mutable UI state:
> 
> - Claude Desktop active window and Tab 3 selection.
> - Sidebar/tab state, now suspected to include a collapsed-sidebar edge case.
> - Composer focus after `Cmd+3`.
> - The destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` / paste sequence only being safe if the composer is focused.
> 
> The hard-stop observation for this ticket says the bridge selected active session text content instead of the input field. That means the failure is specifically prompt-surface targeting, not A2A storage and not simply bridge process startup.
> 
> ### Repair ordering
> 
> 1. Restore and prove the known-good Claude Desktop path first.
> 2. Add a read-only Accessibility tree probe for Claude Desktop if needed to identify the composer under expanded and collapsed sidebar states.
> 3. Only after target proof, allow a non-destructive focus or guarded paste path.
> 4. Evaluate Claude Channels as the strategic Shape A-adjacent replacement lane, but do not block regression recovery on it unless a minimal prototype proves same-session delivery faster.
> 
> This avoids the current trap: letting untrusted Web UI advice turn a concrete regression into speculative architecture churn.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:35:50Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## What changed? Delta against pre-#10607 known-good path
> 
> Short answer: #10607 did **not** directly change active `bridge-daemon.mjs` Claude delivery. It changed the recovery/resume plane. The active bridge failure is more likely caused by changed Claude Desktop runtime topology / focus state exposed by that recovery work.
> 
> ### Direct code delta
> 
> - PR #10607 changed `ai/scripts/resumeHarness.mjs` only, adding the Harness Registry and mapping `@neo-opus-4-7 -> claude-desktop` with `tabShortcut: '3'`.
> - PR #10607 did **not** change `ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.mjs`.
> - Since #10607, the Claude branch in `bridge-daemon.mjs` is still functionally the same: `appName === 'Claude'` => `tabShortcut = '3'`, then `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` / paste / Enter.
> - Current active SQLite subscription confirms the same route: `WAKE_SUB:4df2d514-90ef-4b11-8c96-e25dabb197f3 | @neo-opus-4-7 | bridge-daemon | appName=Claude | tabShortcut=3 | active`.
> 
> The bridge-daemon changes after #10607 are not Claude-focus changes:
> 
> - #10639 captures osascript stderr.
> - #10652 changes Antigravity from `Cmd+L` to `Cmd+Shift+I` and adds chord expansion. Claude remains `Cmd+3`.
> 
> ### Runtime/topology delta
> 
> The meaningful change after #10607 is that the recovery plane started manipulating Claude Desktop sessions/windows:
> 
> - #10607 added the Claude Desktop resume route.
> - #10619 turned resume into Q1b fresh-session spawn (`Cmd+N` + boot-grounding prompt).
> - #10641/#10642 then proved the staleness predicate caused unsanctioned fresh-session spawns during rate-limit / active-session windows.
> - #10647 documents three orphan Claude Desktop sessions plus prompt-landing failures as part of the same incident class.
> 
> That matters because `bridge-daemon.mjs` does not target a specific Claude window, session, or composer. It activates the app, asks System Events for the frontmost process, sends `Cmd+3`, then immediately runs destructive selection/cut/paste against whatever element owns focus.
> 
> ### Current best hypothesis
> 
> The invariant that broke is:
> 
> > `Cmd+3` selects Code tab => prompt composer is focused.
> 
> That was true enough in the previous UI/session state. It is not true in the hard-stop reproduction: the bridge selected active session text content, which means `Cmd+3` reached or kept focus on transcript/content instead of composer. The collapsed-sidebar state and/or orphan/fresh sessions likely changed the post-activation focus target.
> 
> So this is still a regression: the pre-#10607 known-good path was real, but it was relying on an implicit focus invariant. #10607/#10619 changed the surrounding recovery/session topology enough to expose that invariant as false.
> 
> ### Next forensic check
> 
> Before any paste path runs again, add a read-only probe that records after `activate + Cmd+3`:
> 
> - Claude window/session count and selected window title if accessible.
> - Focused UI element role/subrole/description/value shape.
> - Whether sidebar is expanded/collapsed.
> - Whether the focused element is the composer, not transcript/content.
> 
> If composer proof is absent, fail closed before `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X`. This is the missing guard between the known-good old route and the current failure.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:37:45Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Operator correction: `Cmd+3` is stable tab focus, not prompt focus
> 
> @tobiu corrected the key invariant:
> 
> > `Cmd+3` focuses the Claude Code tab. Stable. It does **not** focus the prompt field automatically.
> 
> This sharpens the regression diagnosis.
> 
> ### Corrected model
> 
> - `Cmd+3` remains valid as the Claude Desktop Code-tab selector.
> - The missing primitive is the **second step**: focus the Claude Code prompt/composer inside the already-selected Code tab.
> - The old bridge path worked only when the prompt happened to already own focus after Code-tab selection. That was an incidental UI-state precondition, not a supported invariant.
> 
> ### Consequence for the bridge
> 
> The current sequence is unsafe for Claude:
> 
> 1. activate Claude
> 2. `Cmd+3` to Code tab
> 3. `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` / paste / Enter
> 
> Step 2 is necessary but insufficient. Step 3 must not run unless a prompt-focus proof has happened after Step 2.
> 
> ### Updated repair target
> 
> #10658 should implement or discover a Claude Desktop **composer-focus primitive** after `Cmd+3`, with fail-closed behavior when it cannot be proven. Candidate discovery order:
> 
> 1. Live `Cmd+/` shortcut overlay: look specifically for a non-destructive “focus prompt/composer/input” action in the Code tab.
> 2. Read-only Accessibility tree probe after `activate + Cmd+3`: identify the composer role/description/value shape and whether `AXFocused` can be set safely.
> 3. If no stable focus primitive exists, refuse automated paste and keep Claude wake delivery disabled until Channels/CLI/session targeting proves same-session delivery.
> 
> This means `tabShortcut: '3'` remains correct but should be renamed or treated semantically as `tabSelectShortcut`, not `promptFocusShortcut`. The bridge currently conflates those two concepts.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:40:55Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Operator observation: printable key focuses prompt, `Cmd+A` does not
> 
> @tobiu added a crucial runtime observation:
> 
> > In Claude Desktop Code tab, typing any normal key focuses the input field, e.g. typing `r`. But starting with `Cmd+A` can select text inside the chat history when the prompt is not already focused.
> 
> This explains the regression very directly.
> 
> ### Root cause refinement
> 
> The existing bridge sequence begins the prompt-prep phase with a destructive selection chord:
> 
> ```text
> Cmd+3 -> Cmd+A -> Cmd+X -> paste wake -> Enter
> ```
> 
> If no prompt focus exists after `Cmd+3`, then `Cmd+A` targets transcript/history content. This is exactly the hard-stop failure: selected active session text content instead of the input field.
> 
> ### Candidate minimal fix shape
> 
> For Claude only, insert a non-destructive printable focus seed after `Cmd+3` and before any selection chord:
> 
> ```text
> Cmd+3 -> type harmless printable sentinel -> prompt gains focus -> Cmd+A -> Cmd+X -> paste wake -> Enter
> ```
> 
> The sentinel must be chosen and handled carefully:
> 
> - It should be a normal printable character that reliably moves focus into the composer.
> - It must be removed before wake payload paste (`Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` after focus should remove it).
> - It must not submit or trigger a slash-command / mode switch / cancellation path.
> - It should be covered by an adapter-intent test so future changes keep the sentinel before `Cmd+A`.
> 
> ### Safety note
> 
> This is still not full proof of prompt landing; it is a stronger focus primitive than `Cmd+3` alone, based on live operator observation. The bridge should still fail closed when the matrix cannot verify prompt landing. But this gives #10658 a plausible minimal regression fix without jumping to Channels/CLI first.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:46:13Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Operator observation: Space can focus without writing; AX focus detection is weak
> 
> @tobiu added a new observation:
> 
> > In Claude Code Tab 3, pressing a normal key focuses the input field. Space is the safest candidate because when the prompt is not focused, Space focuses the input but does not write a character. Starting with `Cmd+A` is unsafe because it can select chat-history text if prompt focus is absent.
> 
> I also ran read-only Accessibility probes against the live Claude process from Codex/Terminal. Results:
> 
> - `AXFocusedWindow` is exposed and returns `AXWindow | title=Claude`.
> - `AXFocusedUIElement` exists as a process attribute but returns `missing value` after activation.
> - A read-only scan of `entire contents of window 1` returned only generic `AXGroup` entries and window buttons; no element reported `focused=true`, and no prompt/composer role was visible through simple System Events AppleScript.
> 
> ### Implication
> 
> Simple AppleScript/System Events can currently tell us that Claude is active and which window is focused, but it does **not** reliably expose prompt-focus state. A lower-level AX probe may still be worth testing, but the basic bridge-script layer should not assume it can detect composer focus directly.
> 
> ### Updated candidate repair shape
> 
> For Claude Desktop only:
> 
> ```text
> activate Claude -> Cmd+3 -> Space focus seed -> Cmd+A -> Cmd+X -> paste wake -> Enter
> ```
> 
> This is better than starting with `Cmd+A` because Space is non-destructive when focus is in the transcript/history state described by @tobiu.
> 
> Residual risk: if the prompt is already focused and contains a user draft, Space may insert a character before the cut/preserve flow. The implementation should either tolerate this as a much smaller residual than transcript selection, or add a follow-up mitigation once we can prove cursor/draft state. The #10649 matrix should explicitly test both cases:
> 
> 1. Code tab selected, transcript/history focused, prompt empty.
> 2. Code tab selected, prompt already focused, prompt empty.
> 3. Code tab selected, prompt already focused, prompt contains a draft.
> 
> No Claude bridge reactivation should happen until this is proven live.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-05-03T18:49:50Z

**Input from GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop):**

> ● ## Focused-prompt AX retry: no useful delta
> 
> @tobiu manually focused the Claude Code prompt field, then I reran the same read-only Accessibility probes.
> 
> Results while prompt was expected to be focused:
> 
> - `AXFocusedUIElement` still returned `missing value`.
> - `AXFocusedWindow` still returned `AXWindow | title=Claude`.
> - A scan for child elements with `focused=true` returned `found=0`.
> 
> This means the basic System Events / AppleScript layer still cannot distinguish prompt-focused from prompt-unfocused for Claude Desktop Code tab.
> 
> ### Implication
> 
> Direct focus detection is not available through the simple AX path we can use inside `bridge-daemon.mjs` today. The repair should not wait on `AXFocusedUIElement` unless someone implements a deeper native AX probe and proves it returns a stable composer signal.
> 
> The best minimal same-session repair candidate remains the operator-observed focus seed:
> 
> ```text
> activate Claude -> Cmd+3 -> Space -> Cmd+A -> Cmd+X -> paste wake -> Enter
> ```
> 
> Rationale:
> 
> - `Cmd+3` reliably selects Claude Code tab.
> - Space focuses the prompt when the transcript/history owns focus and, per operator observation, does not write a character in that state.
> - Once Space has transferred focus, `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` operates on the prompt instead of chat history.
> 
> Residual risk remains: prompt already focused with a human draft. That needs explicit matrix coverage before bridge reactivation.
